task_completion.description: Verify that three distinct segmentation masks (Brain, Bone, Fat) have been created with the specified HU ranges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius GUI, look at the 'Masks' panel (usually on the bottom left). There should be at least three masks listed. The threshold tool might show the ranges (-100 to 80, >=600, -300 to -20) being applied, or the 2D slices should show colored overlays corresponding to these distinct tissues.

task_completion.description: Verify that 3D surfaces have been generated from the three masks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Surfaces' panel in InVesalius. There should be three surfaces listed, corresponding to the masks. The 3D viewer window (usually bottom right) should display the generated 3D models (e.g., skull structure, brain shape).

task_completion.description: Verify that at least 5 linear measurements have been added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D slice viewers or the 3D viewer in InVesalius. There should be at least 5 distinct measurement lines with distance values displayed on them. Alternatively, check if there is a 'Measurements' panel listing at least 5 items.

task_completion.description: Verify that the three 3D surfaces were exported as STL files to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Export 3D surface' dialog box saving files as 'brain_tissue.stl', 'skull_bone.stl', and 'periorbital_fat.stl' in '/home/ga/Documents/rt_planning/', or a file manager/terminal window showing these three files in that directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the InVesalius project was saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save Project As' dialog box saving 'rt_tissue_atlas.inv3' in '/home/ga/Documents/rt_planning/', or the InVesalius window title bar showing 'rt_tissue_atlas.inv3', or a file manager showing the file.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the InVesalius application open with active interactions (clicking menus, adjusting thresholds, drawing measurements).

integrity.description: Verify that the data being worked on is a genuine cranial CT scan.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 2D viewers in InVesalius should display cross-sections of a human head (skull, brain tissue, eyes/fat).

integrity.description: Verify that the outputs were not generated via terminal scripts bypassing the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing python scripts or shell commands being used to mock the .stl or .inv3 files directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CT Cranium dataset contains brain tissue (-100 to 80 HU), compact bone (>= 600 HU), and periorbital fat (-300 to -20 HU). The task requires creating three distinct masks for these tissues, generating 3D surfaces for each, exporting them as 'brain_tissue.stl', 'skull_bone.stl', and 'periorbital_fat.stl' to '/home/ga/Documents/rt_planning/', adding at least 5 linear measurements, and saving the project as 'rt_tissue_atlas.inv3' in the same directory.
